text:
This year I've jumped tax bracket (15% 25%), thanks having real job (not graduate student stipend) and working real hard it. But had idea how dip back into the lower tax bracket saving more for retirement.
Say have $40,000 taxable income this year. The 25% tax bracket starts $34,000. figured that opened retirement account that uses pre-tax dollars (like individual 401K), could claim that deduction. contributed $6,001 that fund, I'd deduct that and have taxable income $33,999, putting the 15% tax bracket. Instead owing 25% $40,000 (which $10,000), I'd owe 15% $33,999 (which $5,100). I'd have locked $6,001 save $4,900.
asked some people about this scheme.
read some money blogs (investing for retirement, frugality, etc.) and decided ask one them about scheme. wrote blog post answering question (and renamed me), but didn't really address the scheminess scheme. Sure, it's great save more for retirement, and doesn't make much sense have two IRAs Roth and Traditional), but would really reduce tax burden dramatically? No.
didn't understand how tax brackets work. thought that I'd pay 15% all $33,999 taxable income (total=$5,100) 25% all $34,000 taxable income (total=$8,500), with middle ground. But it's not like that. EVERYBODY pays 10% the first $8,375, then 15% the next $25,625, then 25% any amount over that, and forth.
had carried out scheme, tax burden (on $33,999) would $4,681. Without opening the new retirement account, I'd have taxable income $40,000, and tax burden $6,181. would really only save $1,500, which what you'd expect from $6,000 deduction the 25% tax bracket.
realize this may jibberish. The point that there isn't much gained (or lost) jumping tax brackets. It's not that big deal.
I'll probably open 401K anyway. doesn't hurt save money when possible.

was wondering what sort BHP and Torque needed get in.
Say have early panel, there interior, straight axle and lowered (if that makes some difference E/T times air resistance).
know gearbox ratios and tyre size will affect performance, but there must minimum amount energy needed get the times needed.
Looking engine options, could with some advice. already have type with twin 40's IDF (both under the workbench). also have AS41 case.
Approx 120 plus horsepower what's needed.
had type with set 40's and good BAS exhaust and just scraped with 17.8 thought would lot quicker than that.
Reckon with stripped out panel you may get away with little less power but not much unless you're going put really short ration box it.
Cheers Orb, take that 120 the flywheel. What sort torque did your engine produce?
Yep, 120 the flywheel.
Not sure what torque made, never had the dyno the 120bhp bit "seat the pants" dyno having had number fast motors and knowing what sort times others have run and what power they're making.
Careful you will get hooked! started with 1835cc motor and couldn't get near the 18s iirc went 2332, then 2332 with fuel injection and turbo and now I'm nitrous also probably about 450bhp plus want.
struggled get under for quite while looked easy but soon found quite skill getting everything come together.
Five years down the line I'm chasing 12s, 14.1 without nitrous and being conservative off the line.
That's ultimate goal anyway.
tufty65 wrote: Careful you will get hooked!
Thanks, well have type from 1700 fuel injection 411, did have litre heads barrels and pistons etc. but sold then when was skint!. right thinking that the 1700 puts out about 75hp, and the litre 90ish. sounds like you have tune litre quite bit get the times!!
the early 90's had bay that put litre that had tuned (well polished the heads and threw together). used really well (erm drank petrol), until spat out valve guide 80...Going have build something properly this time.
The Porsche 914 1700cc put out 80hp but the Bus 1700cc only 66HP, and again the Porsche 2.0 made 94HP but the 2.0 bus engine only 69HP.
it's all the engine code and the fuel incjection system. Some 412s had the same engine the VW/Porsche 914.
This pushed beetle personal best 16.01 with the belt on, and 15.53 with the Belt off.
